task_completion.description: Verify that the patient record for John Zenon is opened in GNU Health.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GNU Health client should display John Zenon's patient form, dashboard, or medical record.

task_completion.description: Verify that an occupational risk factor is documented using an ICD-10 code starting with Z57.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A condition, disease, or evaluation record for John Zenon showing an ICD-10 code beginning with 'Z57' (e.g., Z57.3 or similar for occupational exposure to dust).

task_completion.description: Verify that a clinical evaluation documents baseline Respiratory Rate and Oxygen Saturation (SpO2).
task_completion.visual_evidence: A clinical evaluation or vitals entry screen showing numerical values entered into the 'Respiratory Rate' and 'Oxygen Saturation' (or SpO2) fields.

task_completion.description: Verify that a Lifestyle record is created to document the patient's smoking history.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Lifestyle tab or a Lifestyle record for John Zenon showing entries or selections related to smoking history/status.

task_completion.description: Verify that at least two baseline laboratory tests are ordered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A laboratory request or orders screen showing at least two distinct lab tests (such as CBC and Comprehensive Metabolic Panel) ordered for John Zenon.

task_completion.description: Verify that a follow-up appointment is scheduled approximately one year (350 to 380 days) from the current date.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An appointment record or calendar view showing a scheduled appointment for John Zenon with a date approximately one year in the future.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent interacted with the GNU Health graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the GNU Health desktop client being used, not terminal commands (like psql) or API scripts.

integrity.description: Verify that the modifications were made to the correct patient.
integrity.visual_evidence: The name 'John Zenon' should be visible on the records being modified. Ensure the agent did not modify the distractor patient 'Ana Betz'.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent actually entered the data into the system forms and saved them.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show forms being filled out and saved within the GNU Health application, rather than text typed into a notepad or search bar.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target patient is exactly 'John Zenon'. The occupational risk factor must use an ICD-10 code starting with 'Z57'. At least 2 new lab orders must be created. The follow-up appointment must be scheduled between 350 and 380 days from the current date. Ensure changes are made to John Zenon and not the distractor patient Ana Betz.
